Okay, so now like I said earlier, I'm a very much a long-term strategic thinker. So I want to walk you through this because I think this is where a lot of the real value is. So what did I want from this program? I wanted high quality people. I wanted to enhance my brand. This brand's worth a lot of money to me. I didn't want to screw that up. Very, very important to me. I wanted to maintain the perceived value of product launch formula. Product launch formula cost $2,000. So if I was going to train people a super set of this, they would have to know product launch formula plus a whole other piece of how to manage, attract, manage clients. This is a bigger piece. So product launch formula is $2,000. This had to be at least, it had to be more than that. And it had to be significantly more. It had to be at least four grand. So I'm like, okay, this has to be a $4,000 program. But I don't like that price. So I said, we're gonna make it $5,000. Because I figure if you can get $4,000 from someone, you can get $5,000. Does that seem, I mean, okay. So I decided it's gotta be five grand. Am I gonna take a cut of their business? I don't know, couldn't figure that one out. That was hard to figure out. I just walked by that question. I was gonna have to make a lot of money for it to interest me. And if I was gonna sell for five grand, and I'm like an online guy, I am not like shipping, there's been times where I've shipped CDs and books, but I didn't wanna do that. I wanted to do it completely online, deliver it as an online program. And so how do you sell a bunch of videos? I mean, you can go to YouTube and get a lot of videos for free. How do you just sell a bunch of videos for five grand? Okay, I'm gonna need some real firepower to do this. And then I looked at my assets. This is a process that I always go through. It's like, what are my assets here? So I had a strong personal reputation, product launch formula, I had a great reputation as a product. I had these crazy successes with product launch formula that I'd showed the market over the years. I had product launch formula that's established on a $2,000 product. I had some great joint venture partners that I knew would promote for me because they make a fortune when they promote for me. I knew I had hyper responses on my list. And this is something I go to a lot. Every one of you has hyper responses on your list that are really love what you do. I mean, all of us are hyper responses for Dan. Otherwise we wouldn't be here, right? We are on his hyper responsive list. Every list has got those. And I often like, how can I leverage those hyper responses? Because beyond just money, there are other ways and you're gonna see how I did that. I'm also, I run very good events. I'm good at teaching from the stage. And there was huge pent up demand for me to run people's launches. So I dumped all these into the mix. This is part of the magic sauce. Okay, the dangers I identified. No one had ever sold an online course like this at five grand. I put four because of my minimum, but I knew I wanted five. I was inventing a profession, good and bad to that. There's no proven demand for this product launch manager. I just had an inkling that was out there. I was putting my reputation with my partners online. This is a big one because my partners love me because I make them a ton of money. When they promote for me, they know my product converts. They make a ton of money. But if I come out and roll out something and it doesn't make much money, then I've screwed the pooch. That's bad. So the first, you're only as good as your last hit. And so I didn't wanna, it's like five grand, that's a risk. But it's one thing to run that risk to your list and screw up. It's another thing to run it to your JV's list and screw up. That's bad. You send a few emails out to your list, put together a promotion for your list, you mess up, oh, bummer, I really wanted to make that money, but I didn't just destroy my brand, just throw my reputation. Partners, completely different. Very, it's a highway act. Potential loss of control of the brand. I grab a lot of people who are all of a sudden now out there saying they were Jeff Walker's this and Jeff Walker that. I told them they couldn't say they were Jeff Walker certified because the next line is legal stuff. Where are you guys, Dixon Nanton? Yeah, these guys told me I shouldn't call it certified. So they put the fear of God in me. And I'm like, okay, no certified, but then I still had these people running around saying they were certified, so I had to chase them down. So there's potential loss of control of the brand. There was the legal stuff, which thankfully, that conversation was 18 months ago and I don't ever have it again. I'm just not a big fan of legal stuff. And it was a fundamentally different product and different market than I would. I was selling to business owners about how to launch their product. Now I'm selling to people like, here, drop your profession and go into, start a new profession that I just invented. And pay me five grand for it. Okay, so those are some of the dangers identified. So my strategy was I was gonna two-step it. I've just always loved the concept of two-step, right from those little classified ads to get the free report. So I did my version of the two-step here. And what I did was I started with what we call an internal launch. So that was the launch to my list. And like I said, that's always a lot less risky to run it to your list. I started with a very high-end event because, well, I think it's a lot easier to sell a very high-end event than it is to sell a very high-priced series of online videos to download. So I started with a really high-end event. I followed that with a joint venture launch of the home study version. This is the one for the show, two for the dough. So I also love keeping people guessing about what my motives are. So what I did was, and this is the part of the adventure, so I don't know what's on the next slide here. Okay, here we go, step one. So if I'm gonna sell a $5,000 series of videos online, like I said, I needed firepower. So I love the idea of setting up the apples to oranges comparison. I sold a live event, it's a $25,000 event. I figure if I'm gonna end up selling a $5,000 product, if I say, well, this is the home study version of A, then it's much better if it was $25,000, right? And I knew I had those hyper-responses on my list. So those hyper-responses just love to buy and they love to pay high prices. So I was gonna leverage those hyper-responses.
